summary: Score holds at 3.8/5. No new regressions detected since #22, but no fixes landed either. The persistent backlog is now genuinely embarrassing — tooltip keyboard access is on its 6th flag, DEFAULT_ROSTER extraction on its 7th, mobile header overflow on its 5th, card flip tabbing on its 5th. These are all small-effort fixes that keep getting deprioritized. The aria-live screen reader spam and timezone greeting issues from #22 also remain unfixed. At this point the backlog IS the review.

suggestions.title: CSS tooltip only triggers on :hover — keyboard users see nothing (6TH FLAG)
suggestions.description: Office.css: data-tooltip renders via CSS ::after on :hover only. Keyboard users who tab to characters get focus-visible outlines but NEVER see the tooltip. Add :focus-within alongside :hover on the tooltip selector. ONE LINE OF CSS. This has been flagged in reviews #18, #19, #20, #21, and #22. Six reviews. One line. Please.

suggestions.title: Mobile header overflow at 375px — elements clip off-screen (5TH FLAG)
suggestions.description: At 375px viewport, .header-right doesn't wrap. Vibe pill, sync button, streak counter, and theme toggle extend past the screen edge. The @media (max-width: 600px) query reduces gap but never adds flex-wrap: wrap. Add flex-wrap: wrap to .header-right in the 600px media query. Flagged in reviews #19, #20, #21, and #22.

suggestions.title: aria-live='polite' on workers-grid announces ALL workers every 30s poll
suggestions.description: App.jsx: <div className='workers-grid' aria-live='polite'> causes every worker card to be announced to screen readers on every 30s poll cycle. Replace with a dedicated visually-hidden live region that only announces delta events (worker joined/left/status changed). This is actively hostile to AT users.

suggestions.title: Greeting timezone hardcoded to America/Los_Angeles
suggestions.description: App.jsx: currentHour uses timeZone: 'America/Los_Angeles'. Users outside PST get wrong greeting. Use new Date().getHours() for the user's local time. One-line fix.

suggestions.title: Card flip back-face close button tabbable when front showing (5TH FLAG)
suggestions.description: WorkerCard back-face close button remains tabbable even when !isFlipped. aria-hidden suppresses it from AT tree but keyboard tab still reaches it. Add tabIndex={isFlipped ? 0 : -1}. Flagged since review #19.

suggestions.title: Activity filter buttons use aria-pressed but behave as exclusive radio group
suggestions.description: App.jsx: filter bar uses aria-pressed on mutually exclusive buttons. Correct pattern is role='radiogroup' on container and role='radio' + aria-checked on each button. Misleads screen reader users.

suggestions.title: DEFAULT_ROSTER, VIBE_QUOTES, DESKS still in Office.jsx (7TH FLAG)
suggestions.description: Office.jsx: ~60 lines of static data (DEFAULT_ROSTER, VIBE_QUOTES, VIBE_WHITEBOARD, DESKS) defined inline. ROLE_EMOJIS/ROLE_COLORS correctly live in utils/constants.js. Move these too. Consistency matters. Flagged since review #17.

suggestions.title: CharacterAvatar and Character missing React.memo (5TH FLAG)
suggestions.description: Office.jsx: CharacterAvatar is a pure function of props but recreated every render. Every 30s poll re-renders ALL characters. Wrap in React.memo(). Free win, zero behavioral change. Flagged since review #19.

suggestions.title: Office floor min-height gets TALLER at narrower viewports
suggestions.description: Office.css: @media (max-width: 600px) sets min-height: 320px but @media (max-width: 480px) sets 350px. Floor gets taller as screen shrinks — opposite of intended. Fix the 480px rule to 280px or remove it.

suggestions.title: Vibe streak 'x7' has no label or tooltip (4TH FLAG)
suggestions.description: App.jsx: vibe streak span renders bare 'x7' with no title, aria-label, or context. First-time users have no idea what it means. Add title='Vibe streak: consecutive syncs at this level'. Flagged since review #20.

suggestions.title: tabpanel divs have tabIndex=0 but contain focusable children
suggestions.description: App.jsx: both tabpanel divs have tabIndex={0}. Per WAI-ARIA, this is only appropriate when panel has NO focusable descendants. Both have plenty. Adds unnecessary tab stop. Remove tabIndex.

suggestions.title: Whiteboard text breaks mid-word on narrow mobile (3RD FLAG)
suggestions.description: Office.css: .office-whiteboard at 375px is ~20px wide, causing 'SHIP IT' to break as 'SH IP IT'. Shorten to emoji-only at <=480px or increase width to 7%. Flagged since review #21.
